title: Rinorea spinosa
intro: Rinorea spinosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea spinosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Rinorea spinosa include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis and pneumonia.
